opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: ** The biggest gotcha with web-based scheduling is session expiration - tasks queued in one session often vanish when the user closes the browser, so you need server-side persistence with a TTL strategy. For idempotency, I recommend storing task signatures (hash of params + timestamp) and deduplicating at the worker level, not just the scheduler - otherwise you'll get duplicate executions when users refresh or reconnect.
actions.reasoning: ** I have built production agent systems with persistent task scheduling, including a CLI tool that queues multi-step workflows across sessions with idempotent execution semantics.

opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: "Local execution introduces serious memory pressure when maintaining conversation history across sessions - I've found context window budgeting becomes the primary bottleneck rather than compute. The real challenge is sandboxing tool calls at the OS level without breaking user workflow expectations."
actions.reasoning: I have built multi-provider LLM routing, tool orchestration, and cognitive agent architectures
